events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess a transmitter installation scenario: "transmitter above taps on liquid service". The work is rough-complete. The condition is "installed-defective". We need to identify the component/condition and defect cues. The scenario: transmitter above taps on liquid service. In instrumentation, for liquid service, the transmitter should be mounted below the taps to ensure the impulse lines are filled with liquid and any gas bubbles can rise back to the process. If the transmitter is above the taps, gas can accumulate in the impulse lines, causing measurement errors. This is a common installation defect. So the component is the transmitter or the impulse line arrangement. The defect is that the transmitter is mounted above the taps on liquid service, which is non-compliant with good practice (API RP 551, manufacturer manuals). So decision: fail. Risk: medium or high? Could cause inaccurate measurement, potentially leading to process control issues. I'd say high. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out, so maybe 80-90%. Value: not applicable. Confidence: high. Findings: "Transmitter mounted above taps on liquid service; gas accumulation will cause measurement errors." Actions: "Relocate transmitter below taps or install gas collection chambers." Rationale: "For liquid service, transmitter must be below taps to keep impulse lines liquid-filled and allow gas to vent back to process." References: API RP 551, manufacturer installation manuals. So JSON output accordingly.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Transmitter mounted above taps on liquid service",
    "Gas accumulation in impulse lines will cause measurement errors"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate transmitter below the process taps",
    "Alternatively, install gas collection chambers or seal pots if relocation is not feasible"
  ],
  "rationale": "For liquid service, the transmitter must be mounted below the taps to keep impulse lines liquid-filled and allow any gas to vent back to the process. Mounting above taps traps gas, leading to inaccurate readings.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551",
    "Manufacturer installation manuals"
  ]
}
